title: Remove unsafe-inline from LiveView CSP via nonce plug
prompt: `test_fixtures/csp_nonce_liveview.ex` has a CSP that uses `'unsafe-inline'` in both `script-src` and `style-src` because the developer didn't know how to make LiveView's inline socket script work with strict CSP. Rewrite the router so: (a) a plug generates a per-request nonce via `:crypto.strong_rand_bytes(16) |> Base.encode16/1`, (b) the nonce is assigned to `conn.assigns[:csp_nonce]`, (c) `put_secure_browser_headers` is called with an EMPTY CSP string, (d) a follow-up plug sets a dynamic CSP header using the nonce in both `script-src` and `style-src`. The final CSP must contain `'nonce-...'` (literally, with the interpolated hex value) and must NOT contain `'unsafe-inline'` anywhere. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/router.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — requires knowing the nonce-per-request plug pattern and wiring it through put_secure_browser_headers.
